revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伊勢暦]]


[[File:Jokyo-reki.jpg|thumb|280px|right|Ise Calendar ({{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|qid=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō Calendar|1=Jōkyō calendar}}) by Satō Iori, 1729 ({{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era|1=Kyōhō}} 14) edition. (Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Nature and Science|en|National Museum of Nature and Science|qid=Q74940|lt=National Museum of Nature and Science|1=National Museum of Nature and Science}})]]
[[File:Houryaku-reki.jpg|thumb|280px|right|Ise Calendar ({{ill|Hōryaku calendar|en|Hōryaku calendar|qid=Q2352988|lt=Hōryaku Calendar|1=Hōryaku calendar}}) by Noro Shume, 1755 ({{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōryaku era|1=Hōreki}} 5) edition. (Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Nature and Science|en|National Museum of Nature and Science|qid=Q74940|lt=National Museum of Nature and Science|1=National Museum of Nature and Science}})]]
[[File:Nakagitageki1817.jpg|300px|thumb|right|Ise Calendar ({{ill|Kansei calendar|en|Kansei calendar|qid=Q2355562|lt=Kansei Calendar|1=Kansei calendar}}) by {{ill|Nakakita Geki|ja|中北外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakakita Geki|1=Nakagita Geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30928082}}, 1818 ({{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 15) edition.]]

'''Ise Calendar''' (伊勢暦) refers to the {{ill|Calendar|en|Calendar|qid=Q12132|lt=calendar|1=Calendar}} produced and distributed by {{ill|calendar makers|ja|暦師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar makers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579243|1=Q135579243}} in {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|qid=Q422616|lt=Uji (Ise)|1=Uji (disambiguation)}} and {{ill|Yamada (disambiguation)|en|Yamada (disambiguation)|qid=Q355196|lt=Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada (disambiguation)}}, located in front of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. It is the predecessor of today’s {{ill|Jingū calendar|lt=Jingū calendar|lt=Jingū calendar|ja|神宮暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Calendar|qq=}}.

== Overview ==

The Ise Calendar became known throughout Japan as {{ill|Onshi|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from Ise distributed it along with {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|qid=Q11589469|lt=Jingū Taima|1=Jingū taima}} (sacred talismans from Ise Grand Shrine) at the end of the year. There were around 14 to 20 calendar makers in Yamada ({{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|lt=Gekū (Outer Shrine)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}) depending on the period, and one calendar maker in Uji ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Naikū (Inner Shrine)|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}).

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4), the Onshi system was abolished, and the distribution of the Ise Calendar was also discontinued. In April {{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}, a proclamation from the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} decreed that from {{ill|1883|en|1883|qid=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16), official calendars would be issued by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q687168}}, and thus the publication of the calendar continued under the name "Jingū Calendar."

== List of Ise Calendar Publishers (Calendar Makers) ==

* Ise Naikū
** {{ill|Satō Iori|ja|佐藤伊織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satō Iori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* Yamada, Watarai District, Ise
** {{ill|Asuka Tachi|ja|飛鳥帶刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Segawa Toneri|ja|瀬川舎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segawa Toneri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Segawa Chōdayū|ja|瀬川長大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Segawa Chōdayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Tomita Daini|ja|冨田大貮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomita Daini|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Nakagawa Takumi|ja|中川内匠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa Takumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Nakakita Geki|ja|中北外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakakita Geki|1=Nakagita Geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30928082}}
** {{ill|Nagata Chōdayū|ja|永田長大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Chōdayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Nakamura Sukedayū|ja|中村助大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Sukedayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Nishijima Samon|ja|西嶋左門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishijima Samon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Nomura Shigedayū|ja|野村茂大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Shigedayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Noro Shume|ja|野呂主馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noro Shume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Horita Yamashiro|ja|保利田山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horita Yamashiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Minomaki Shuzan|ja|箕曲主膳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minomaki Shuzan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Minomaki Mondo|ja|箕曲主水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minomaki Mondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Miyazaki Sakon|ja|宮崎左近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Sakon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Muramatsu Sakyo|ja|村松左京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muramatsu Sakyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Morita Hachidayū|ja|森田八大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Hachidayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Yamaguchi Uhyōe|ja|山口右兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Uhyōe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

2025
